sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 19.59 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.19)

statement.latex: Does the group of isometries of$^3$have a free non-abelian subgroup such that every non-trivial element acts without nontrivial fixed points in$^3$? The answer is yes if the field of rational numbers$$is replaced by the field$R$of real numbers (see G.\,Tomkowicz, S.\,Wagon, The Banach--Tarski Paradox, Cambridge Univ. Press, 2016.)

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
